title: Child Passenger Safety Seats - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Parenting & Childcare > Child Passenger Safety Seats 15 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter African American Community 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details BAMBINOS OREGON CHILD SAFETY EQUIPMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides child safety equipment, such as car seats, baby gates, and outlet plugs. All products are ordered upon request. Parents are required to pick up and attend a carseat clinic to insure proper carseat installation. 322 Main Street Suite 200 Dallas, OR 97338 Eligibility: Supplies can only be provided to the child's parent or primary caregiver (not child care providers or extended relatives) and is limited to 2 car seats per child Hours: By appointment Email (503) 623-4618 2 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BUILDING HEALTHY FAMILIES CAR SEAT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides car seat checks with a certified car seat technician as well as installation. Primary caregivers may also purchase a car seat. Child must be present to fit the car seat correctly. 207 NE Park Street Enterprise, OR 97828 Eligibility: Child must be present for service Hours: No standard hours Email (541) 426-9411 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHI ST ANTHONY HOSPITAL CHILD PASSENGER SAFETY SEATS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides child passenger safety services, including seat checks and installation education. Offers car seats and booster seats at discounted rates. 2801 St Anthony Way Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Vary (541) 276-5121 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILIES FIRST PARENT RESOURCE CENTER CHILD PASSENGER SAFETY SEATS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers car seats, help with installation, and an inspection with a Child Passenger Safety Certified Technician. 401 S Canyon Boulevard John Day, OR 97845 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 792-0371 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY NURTURING CENTER FAMILY NURTURING CENTER JACKSON COUNTY LITTLE SPROUTS CLOTHING SWAP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides items such as childrens clothing sizes 0-8, diapers, wipes, toys, car seats, stroller, and food, depending on availabilty. 825 E Main Street Suite A Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-5pm, Friday 9am-noon Email (541) 779-5242 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LAKE COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH CHILD PASSENGER SAFETY SEATS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides car seat inspections, help with installation, and car seats. 1250 S 9th Street Lakeview, OR 97630 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Varies by location Email (541) 947-6045 453 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LAKE DISTRICT HOSPITAL CHILD PASSENGER SAFETY SEATS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides car seat inspections, help with installation, and car seats. 700 S J Street Lakeview, OR 97630 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 947-2114 386 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MARION AND POLK EARLY LEARNING HUB CAR SEAT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps families purchase low-cost car seats and educates on how to safely install them. 2995 Ryan Drive SE Suite 100 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: -If family members qualify for WIC, SNAP, or TANF, they may be eligible to receive a car seat; must be willing to show proof of benefit. -At the car seat check-up event the technician will confirm the need for a new car seat -Seats are only provided to parents or legal guardians who must be present to obtain the seat -Only one seat per child. -The child receiving the seat must be present unless the baby is not born yet. -The vehicle in which the child travels must be brought to the car seat check. Hours: Varies by car seat event Email (503) 485-3219 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH COUNTY HEALTH DEPARTMENT MULTNOMAH COUNTY HEALTH DEPARTMENT NORTH EAST HEALTH CENTER AND DENTAL CLINIC HEALTHY BIRTH INITIATIVE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides home visits, health education, and support groups from pregnancy through the child's second year. Offers reduced-cost car seats, as well as certified assistance in installation. 5329 NE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97211 Eligibility: Home visiting services: Pregnant African American women who are less than 18 weeks gestation. Those over 18 weeks gestation can still participate in support groups and classes. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-6:30pm (503) 988-3387 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OHSU DOERNBECHER CHILDREN'S HOSPITAL TOM SARGENT SAFETY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Dedicated to reducing preventable injuries in children by providing education, training, and a variety of childproofing equipment, safety gates, helmets, and protective gear at below-retail prices. The Tom Sargent Safety Center offers a weekday car seat inspection station by appointment at Doernbecher Children's Hospital. Certified child passenger safety technicians are available to inspect car seats and provide hands-on advice. Low-cost car seats are available for purchase for those who qualify. Can connect families to local car seat and child safety resources if they are outside of the Metro area. Portland Metro only: Can do virtual car seat inspections. The Kohl's Foundation, in partnership with DCH Tom Sargent Safety Center, is supporting a Safe Sleep Program. Through the Cribs for Kids Program, cribs and accompanying sheets are available at a reduced cost for qualifying low-income families. The goal is to help families provide a safe sleep area for their child to reduce SUID and other accidental, sleep-related infant deaths. Halo Sleep Sacks and Swaddlers are also available for purchase. 700 SW Campus Drive Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Safety education: Unrestricted For car seats and cribs, families must have a household income below 185% FPL. Children must be present at appointment or mother must be in 3rd trimester of pregnancy. Hours: Monday-Friday 9:30am-3:30pm (503) 418-5666 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST MARK'S LUTHERAN CHURCH BETHLEHEM CHILDREN’S CLOTHES CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides children’s clothing (sizes preemie through teens), maternity clothing, baby supplies (diapers, wipes, pull-ups, formula, and car seats), and toys, while supplies last. Also accepts donations 5415 SE Powell Boulevard Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Ages 0-17 for clothing; expectant parents Hours: Clothing: By appointment only Donations: Tuesday-Friday 10am-3pm Email (503) 777-1443 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UMATILLA-MORROW COMMUNITY HEAD START PENDLETON WIC CAR SEAT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides car seats to low-income families at a reduced cost. Car seat availability is dependent on community donations. 455 SW 13th Street Room 162 Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Low-income households, WIC and headstart Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm (541) 966-3354 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UMATILLA-MORROW COMMUNITY HEAD START UMATILLA WIC CAR SEAT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides car seats to low-income families at a reduced cost. Car seat availability is dependent on community donations. 1371 3rd Street Umatilla, OR 97882 Eligibility: Low-income households, WIC and headstart Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm (541) 564-6878 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UMATILLA-MORROW COMMUNITY HEAD START CAR SEAT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides car seats to low-income families at a reduced cost. Car seat availability is dependent on community donations. 110 NE 4th Street Hermiston, OR 97838 Eligibility: Low-income households, WIC and headstart Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 564-6878 231 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BAMBINOS OREGON CHILD SAFETY EQUIPMENT BUILDING HEALTHY FAMILIES CAR SEAT PROGRAM CHI ST ANTHONY HOSPITAL CHILD PASSENGER SAFETY SEATS FAMILIES FIRST PARENT RESOURCE CENTER CHILD PASSENGER SAFETY SEATS FAMILY NURTURING CENTER LITTLE SPROUTS CLOTHING SWAP LAKE COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH CHILD PASSENGER SAFETY SEATS LAKE DISTRICT HOSPITAL CHILD PASSENGER SAFETY SEATS MARION AND POLK EARLY LEARNING HUB CAR SEAT PROGRAM MULTNOMAH COUNTY HEALTH DEPARTMENT HEALTHY BIRTH INITIATIVE OHSU TOM SARGENT SAFETY CENTER ST MARK'S LUTHERAN CHURCH BETHLEHEM CHILDREN’S CLOTHES CLOSET UMATILLA-MORROW COMMUNITY HEAD START CAR SEAT PROGRAM UMATILLA-MORROW COMMUNITY HEAD START CAR SEAT PROGRAM UMATILLA-MORROW COMMUNITY HEAD START CAR SEAT PROGRAM
